changes: Cast to your TV from anywhere — casting now works for users outside the server's household. The play command is sent from your browser (which is on the same Wi-Fi as your TV) instead of from the server, which can never reach a device inside another home's network. Allow local network access if your browser asks; Chrome and Edge work best, while iPhone/iPad browsers can't reach TVs yet — use the Plex app there. Casting to devices on the server's own network still works from any browser.
Cast playback now streams from the server's public address instead of its LAN address, so TVs outside the server's network can actually play what you cast.
Cast errors now say what went wrong — wrong network, Plex app closed, or a device that can't be remote-controlled — instead of a cryptic 'The operation was aborted due to timeout'.
The device button shows 'Casting…' while the command is being delivered.
Series tagged 'yt' in Sonarr are now picked up by Tuberr within about 15 minutes — discovery and channel detection moved to their own fast loop instead of waiting for the 6-hour refresh cycle.

Fixed channel auto-detection silently skipping freshly discovered series — detection ran before the series' episode list had been synced from Sonarr, so new series sat without a channel until the next cycle. Episodes now sync first and detection runs in the same pass, so tagging a series in Sonarr goes from tag to matched episodes in one step.

Matched episodes that are monitored and missing in Sonarr are now searched automatically after every match run — Sonarr never searches back-catalog on its own, so previously a matched series sat idle until someone clicked search. Manual match corrections also trigger an immediate search.
Fixed the Torznab RSS feed starving newer series — it now returns the 50 most recently uploaded matched episodes across all series instead of iterating series-by-series into a cap.

Better title matching when video titles carry extra noise — an episode title fully contained in a video title (guest names, console suffixes, series branding) now scores as a strong match, with upload-date sanity keeping lookalike re-uploads out.

Back-catalog searches are now sent to Sonarr in batches of 25 per cycle — a single 200-episode burst also fans out to your regular indexers (Sonarr tags can't exclude untagged indexers from tagged series), which rate-limited Prowlarr and made Sonarr's Activity page crawl. The remainder is picked up by later cycles automatically.
Age-restricted videos can now download — drop a Netscape-format cookies.txt (exported from a signed-in YouTube session, see the yt-dlp wiki) into Tuberr's data dir and it's used for every download.
Fixed the sample systemd unit losing everything after the first space in TUBERR_DOWNLOADS_DIR — the Environment= line is now quoted, so paths like '/NAS/Temp Download/tuberr' work.

Fixed download progress never updating in Sonarr's Activity queue — a yt-dlp flag conflict silently suppressed all progress output, so items sat at 0% until they finished. Live percentage, speed, and ETA now report correctly.
Failed downloads no longer sit in Sonarr's queue at 0% forever — they now report a state Sonarr treats as failed, so Sonarr removes them, blocklists the release, and the queue drains.

Added a YouTube cookies box to the YouTube (Tuberr) section — paste a cookies.txt exported from a signed-in YouTube session to unlock age-restricted videos; previously failed episodes retry automatically once cookies are saved.
Raised parallel downloads from 2 to 6 (tunable via the max_concurrent setting or TUBERR_MAX_CONCURRENT env), so large back-catalogs drain much faster.

Fixed episode titles that repeat the show's branding scoring too low — branding is now stripped from both the episode title and the video title before comparing, so shows like The Bald and the Beautiful ('… with Trixie and Katya' in every TVDB title) match correctly.

Cookies are handled safely for parallel downloads — each download gets its own temporary copy of the cookie jar (yt-dlp writes rotated cookies back to the file it's given, so six downloads sharing one file corrupted the session), and the cookies box now explains to export from a private/incognito window so YouTube doesn't rotate the session out from under Tuberr.

Tuberr is now bundled in the Diskovarr Docker image — it starts with the container and pairs itself in Admin → Connections automatically, so Docker admins never install or configure it by hand. Expose port 9832 for Sonarr, share a downloads volume both containers see at the same path, and opt out with TUBERR_ENABLED=false if you run Tuberr elsewhere.

Added one-click Sonarr wiring — the 'Set up Sonarr' button in the YouTube (Tuberr) section creates the yt-tagged Torznab indexer and qBittorrent-compatible download client in Sonarr (idempotent — safe to click again after changing the Tuberr address). Full setup is now: enable the toggle, add a YouTube API key, click Set up Sonarr.
Moved YouTube series management out of the admin tab bar — the Manage Series view now opens as a modal from the YouTube (Tuberr) section on the Connections page, next to the settings it belongs with.

Added an optional YouTube Root Folder setting — admins with a dedicated YouTube library (e.g. /NAS/YT Videos) can route new YouTube series there; the default keeps them in the same root folder as other shows.

Series tagged 'yt' directly in Sonarr are now discovered automatically — Tuberr creates a mapping for them and auto-detects the source channel by probing candidate channels against the episode list, only committing when the videos verifiably match; undetectable series are flagged for a manual channel pick in Manage Series.

Better matching for series whose TVDB episodes have generic titles ('Episode 11') — scoring shifts to episode numbers and dates, and absolute numbering in titles is understood, so shows like FF7 Machinabridged now match every episode.

YouTube requests are locked to Sonarr everywhere — the request dialog, queue editing, and approvals can no longer reroute them to Overseerr or DUMB, and DUMB's pull-mode polling no longer sees them (it would try to fetch them via debrid and fail).

Added YouTube series support through Sonarr via Tuberr, a new companion service included in the repo: it presents itself to Sonarr as a Torznab indexer plus a qBittorrent-compatible download client and downloads the actual videos with yt-dlp, so Sonarr handles quality, naming, and import exactly like any other release. Series added this way are tagged 'yt' in Sonarr, keeping the YouTube indexer and downloader away from your normal shows.

Added a 'Download via' choice to the request dialog — TV requests going to Sonarr can pick Torrent (default) or YouTube; picking YouTube suggests source channels for the show via YouTube search, and also accepts a pasted channel or video URL.

Added TVDB-only shows to search — text search now merges Sonarr's TVDB lookup with TMDB results, so web series that never made it onto TMDB (most YouTube shows) appear in results and can be requested. Library items are also matched by their TVDB id, so YouTube shows already in Plex are recognized as In Library.

Added automatic episode matching — Tuberr fetches the mapped channel's uploads through the YouTube Data API and scores each video against TVDB episode titles, air dates, episode numbers, and durations; confident matches are used automatically and the rest wait for review.

Added a Manage Series view (opened from the YouTube section on Admin → Connections) to review and correct matches: per-episode confidence badges, a ranked candidate list, paste-a-URL overrides, unmatching, re-running auto-match after a channel change, and a 'Search in Sonarr' button to re-grab an episode after fixing its match.

Added a YouTube (Tuberr) section to Admin → Connections with an enable/disable toggle that gates the whole feature (search results, request option, and series management), the YouTube Data API key, a connection test, and step-by-step setup instructions behind an info icon — including one-click copy of the Tuberr API key needed by Sonarr's indexer. Sonarr credentials and the YouTube key are pushed to Tuberr automatically on save.

Tuberr manages yt-dlp itself — the official standalone binary is downloaded on first start and self-updates daily, so there is nothing to install or keep current (set YTDLP_PATH to opt out and manage your own).

Monitored YouTube series stay current automatically — every 6 hours Tuberr re-syncs episode lists from Sonarr and re-matches new channel uploads, and newly matched episodes flow to Sonarr through its normal RSS sync, so new episodes download without any manual action.

Tuberr writes its API key to data/api_key.txt on boot so first-time setup never requires digging through logs; failed video downloads mark the match as broken so Sonarr blocklists the release and the review UI flags it.

Added Diskovarr Wrapped — a story-style walkthrough of your year, one stat per slide like Spotify Wrapped, each with its own share button: hours watched, top movies and shows, top genres, viewing patterns (month-by-month chart, most active day and hour, biggest binge day, longest streak), percentile rankings, your 'taste age', a 'show buddy', a Diskovarr personality (genre-derived archetype with trait badges), a critic slide for review writers, and your Diskovarr activity. Reached from your own profile; each year unlocks December 1 and stays viewable afterward as an archive, and during December a banner tile on the home page links straight to it. Admins can preview the in-progress year.
Added the Wrapped server leaderboard — everyone's watch hours and plays for the year, with real usernames.

Added Wrapped stat sharing — each stat section generates a share card image (standard and square) with the same share options as reviews, served from unguessable per-user links so stats stay private until shared.
Added a one-click 'Diskovarr Wrapped' Plex playlist built from your top content of the year; re-running it rebuilds the playlist instead of duplicating it.
Added an admin full watch-history backfill that pages through the entire Tautulli history so Wrapped has complete data on fresh installs.
Wrapped counts a play only if it was marked watched or ran at least 5 minutes and reached 20% completion, so accidental clicks never inflate the stats.

Fixed editing a monitor duplicating its criteria on every save — saving now replaces the criteria set in one step, removing a criterion actually persists, and duplicates created by the old bug are cleaned up automatically.

Fixed monitor criteria for keyword, language, and production company never matching newly added Plex content — they're now filled in from cached TMDB details, so those monitors notify on library additions too.

Deletion safety: titles that were deleted and re-added to Plex are no longer treated as never watched by deletion profiles — watch stats fall back to matching by title, and an ambiguous match can only prevent a deletion, never cause one.

Deletion safety: profiles using watch-based criteria (never played, not played in X days, play count) now apply a 14-day minimum age to newly added items when no 'added less than X days ago' exclusion is set, so fresh content can't be deleted before anyone has a chance to watch it. An explicit value you set always wins.

Fixed show deletions falling through to a raw Plex file delete when a transient TMDB lookup failure prevented finding the show in Sonarr — the deletion is now retried on the next run instead of leaving Sonarr monitoring a deleted show.
Auto-request lists now retry a failed sync within about an hour instead of waiting out the full sync interval; the error still shows on the list until a sync succeeds.

Fixed the search suggestions dropdown appearing nearly transparent — it now renders outside the nav bar (whose glass effect disables nested blur in Chromium) and uses the same frosted glass as the user menu.

Fixed real-time Plex detection — newly added items are now picked up the moment Plex finishes processing them, so requested titles are marked available and join the library within seconds instead of waiting for the next periodic library rescan.
Fixed 'now available' notifications — users are now notified when a title they requested is added to the library; a request-matching bug meant these notifications never fired from library scans.

Fixed the Watched count on the admin Users page never updating — the per-user Plex watched lookup was silently failing when the server rejected stored user tokens, so counts only reflected Tautulli data. Watched syncs now also pull each user's watch history directly from the Plex server, and counts update as users watch.

Movies can now be reviewed from your watch history once you've watched more than 10% of them — no need to finish first. Shows are unchanged: any watched episode qualifies. Rows under 10% show a disabled Write Review button with a hint.
Fixed the default request app chosen in Admin → Connections being ignored when DUMB is enabled in pull mode — your selected default (Overseerr, DUMB, or Sonarr/Radarr) is now always honored.
Fixed choosing an alternate request app from the Advanced option — selecting Radarr, Sonarr, or Overseerr now actually submits to that app instead of being silently dropped under DUMB pull mode.

Fixed the alternate request app list offering services that can't handle the title — Radarr no longer appears for shows and Sonarr no longer appears for movies, including in the admin Edit Request dialog.
DUMB is now labeled 'DUMB' instead of 'Riven' wherever it appears as a request option.

Fixed the accent color reverting to the default when returning to the app from the admin panel — your saved color now applies instantly on every page load with no flash, and a cached theme response can no longer override a fresh change.
Fixed the notification dropdown appearing nearly transparent — it now uses the same frosted-glass effect as the user menu.
Fixed the Settings page on mobile — the section tabs now scroll horizontally instead of overflowing the screen, with spacing tuned for small screens.
UI localization — full interface translation for Spanish, French, German, and Portuguese with user-selectable language in settings.
Automation tab — new admin panel tab for automating library management with auto requests, auto collections, and auto deletion profiles.
Health endpoint — new /health route for external monitoring, load balancers, and orchestrators.
Graceful shutdown — server drains active connections and flushes pending writes before exiting, preventing data corruption on restarts.
Automated DB backups — configurable scheduled backups of the SQLite database with retention policies.

Security hardening — session cookies are now marked Secure over HTTPS, API keys no longer create persistent sessions, the poster proxy blocks path traversal, and compute-heavy endpoints are rate limited.
Fixed stale results when changing filters mid-search or while recommendations were still building.
Performance and code improvements — faster Requests page rendering, new database indexes, cached Plex device lookups, and a new automated test suite.
Fixed queue page filters that were resetting unexpectedly due to broken URL-based filter state.
Fixed bulk delete of multiple requests and issues — transaction handling was preventing deletes from completing.
Fixed Plex SSE integration — message parsing now handles both SSE eventsource and WebSocket endpoint shapes, restoring real-time new-content detection.
User profiles — each user now has a profile with a customizable bio, their reviews, and a personal watch history page.
Reviews and community — a dedicated Reviews page with social interaction, plus reviews displayed on user profiles.
Shareable review images — generate beautifully formatted review images and share them to popular social media and messaging platforms.
Most Popular on Server — new sections on the Diskovarr page surface trending content within your own Plex ecosystem.
Cast and Crew tab — the item details modal now has a Cast and Crew tab for exploring actors, directors, and production staff.
Ratings integration — TMDB and Plex rating integration; ratings submitted in Diskovarr now sync back to Plex.
User monitors — create personalized monitors based on content criteria and get notified when newly available content matches your rules.
Synced library selection — admins can choose which Plex libraries are synchronized into Diskovarr.
Expanded Filter page — enhanced filtering capabilities and search refinement options.
Watchlist and Blacklist moved from the user menu into user profiles for better organization.
Improved stale-content pruning so deleted or changed media is automatically removed and kept in sync.
Added a Blacklist page where users can see and manage blacklisted items.
Added a site footer with GitHub and other links.
Broadcast notification font styles are now applied correctly in the notification bell and modal.
Broadcast font-style buttons now highlight to reflect their enabled state.
Admins can now edit approved requests to change the request app or season selection.
Item info modals now open when selecting an item from the Queue page.
Watchlist now correctly syncs and removes watched and deleted items.

Full React + Express rewrite — the server-rendered EJS UI has been replaced with a modern React SPA backed by the existing Express API. All existing data, sessions, and admin settings are preserved on upgrade.
Broadcast notification editor — rich-text editor with font-style options: bold, italic, strikethrough, and inline code, with per-channel markdown stripping for Discord and Pushover delivery.
Queue and Issues — server-side search and filtering. New API endpoints support search by title, user filter, and date-range filter across the full result set rather than just the current page.

Plex SSE integration — recently-added media is now detected via Plex Server-Sent Events for faster and more reliable new-content notifications, complementing the WebSocket sync introduced in v1.17.11.
Bulk-select on Queue and Issues — themed checkboxes with bulk-delete actions for clearing multiple rows in a single click.
DUMB/Riven torrent browser — search any title, browse Torrentio results with Real-Debrid cache status, and inject a torrent directly into Riven from the admin panel.
Overseerr-compatible API expansion — significantly broader compatibility surface (40+ new endpoints) for Agregarr, DUMB, and Homarr.
Personalized home hero — greeting now uses your username rendered in your chosen accent color.
Nav menu alignment fix — desktop FAB menu positioning corrected to properly align with the user button.
Sign in to Plex button — the Connections → Plex page now has a Sign in to Plex button that opens a Plex OAuth flow and automatically retrieves and fills in your admin token.
User settings persistence fix — per-user settings were not being carried over correctly in certain scenarios and are now reliably saved and applied.
Plex real-time sync — WebSocket fully fixed and operational. Plex webhook integration removed; new-content detection now runs entirely over the persistent WebSocket.

Recommendation engine — episode count weighting: TV shows watched more of now score higher. Multiplier up to ×2.0 based on log₁₀(episodes watched). Shows watched ≥90% to completion always receive the full ×2.0 signal regardless of total episode count.

Unified Overseerr Compat API key — replaced separate DUMB and Agregarr keys with a single shared key in Admin → General. Enable toggle and service accounts moved from Connections to General Settings. Legacy keys remain valid until Regenerate is used.
Agregarr removed from Connections tab — configuration now entirely in Admin → General → Overseerr Compat API.
Regenerating the compat key now also disables old DUMB/Agregarr-type app entries, ensuring only one valid key after regeneration.
Generate/Regenerate button now reads 'Generate Key' when no key exists and 'Regenerate Key' (red) once set — applies to both the Diskovarr API Key and Overseerr Compat Key.
Overseerr shim — media.status now returns 5 (Available) for requests whose content is already in the Plex library. Homarr media-requests-list widget now shows correct availability.
Overseerr shim — /request/count now includes movie and tv breakdown counts.

Overseerr shim — added GET /request/:id, GET /user/:id/requests, GET /user/:id/quota, GET /user/:id/watchlist, GET /status, GET /status/appdata, GET /media, GET /media/:id, settings endpoints, and notification settings stubs.
Copy icon fix — copy buttons on API key fields now show a clipboard icon instead of the eye icon.

Documentation updated — diskovarr.com guide reflects unified compat key, updated Agregarr and DUMB setup flows, reverse proxy sections removed from install guides, all Riven/DUMB references updated to DUMB/Riven.
Watched status on tiles and modals — items you have already watched show a green checkmark badge on the poster thumbnail and a Watched pill in the detail modal.
Queue — Available filter button shows all approved requests that are now in your Plex library.
Queue — sortable column headers — clicking Title, User, Type, Age, or Status sorts the full queue server-side across all pages with an arrow indicator.
Queue — larger posters — request row poster thumbnails increased from 40×60 to 52×78 px.
README — Docker Hub install instructions added to the Installation section.

Fix: Watched count showing ~715 for all users — Tautulli history was being fetched with Plex user IDs not present in Tautulli, returning full shared history. Sync now validates user IDs against Tautulli’s user list and skips unknown users.
Fix: Content-available notifications now open the item modal for that title instead of navigating to the queue.
Fix: Read notifications in the bell dropdown are now clickable.
Fix: Notification modal poster not loading — full HTTPS image URLs were being wrapped in the Plex poster proxy, causing them to fail. HTTP/HTTPS URLs are now passed through directly.
Fix: Torrent browser — Browse Torrents page returned a 500 error on load due to a missing bgGradientCss variable not being passed to the view from the riven route.
Fix: Admin panel DUMB/Riven wording — section headers, toggle labels, default service button, and mode descriptions updated to lead with DUMB throughout the admin panel.
Documentation site — full setup guide and documentation now live at diskovarr.com. Covers installation, all admin panel settings, every page, integrations, and the full API reference.
Documentation link in admin panel — a Documentation pill in the admin panel version strip links directly to the docs site, visible on all tabs.

Fix: Cast to TV device isolation — GDM (UDP broadcast) and PMS /clients removed as device discovery sources; both ran on the server’s local network, exposing the admin’s Plex players to all users. Device lookup now uses plex.tv/api/v2/resources and devices.xml with each user’s own token so every user sees only their own registered devices.

Fix: Cast to TV remote casting — playMedia was being sent with the server admin token, which cannot control another user’s Plex client. The command is now sent with the requesting user’s own token. Connection lookup also prefers relay connections (work from any network) over local LAN connections, fixing cast failures for users on a different network than the server.

Per-user default landing page — users can choose Diskovarr or Diskovarr Requests as their landing page in My Settings. Admins can set this per-user too. Removed the server-wide landing page setting from the admin panel.
Request count in admin user table — the Users tab now shows a Requests column with each user's total request count.

Admin users pagination — the Users tab paginates at 10 per page (configurable to 25 or 50 via a dropdown). The per-page selector is always visible and pagination loads in place without a full page reload.

Fix: Season limit bypass — service users with a per-season limit could bypass it because the cost was based on the explicit seasons list. Automated tools always send seasons:[1], so every show counted as 1 season. The limit now charges the full TMDB season count and stores it in the DB so the rolling window stays accurate.
Fix: Season bubbles overflowing on queue page — shows with more than 9 seasons selected now truncate to a ... ellipsis bubble followed by the last 8 seasons.

Fix: Experimental cast — GDM discovery now broadcasts to the Plex multicast address in addition to broadcast. The cast endpoint now correctly creates a PlayQueue before sending playMedia. Direct player connection lookup added as primary cast path before falling back to PMS relay.
Nav logo size — the SVG logo in the top-left nav bar is larger and more proportional to the Diskovarr wordmark.

Fix: Show/hide eye icons on API key fields — the toggle icon was invisible on connection fields that use empty <button> elements; all fields now show a consistent eye icon matching the Riven/Agregarr fields.

Connections tab cleanup — the Riven/DUMB and Agregarr instruction boxes have been moved into ⓘ info modals next to the section headers; the DUMB request mode selector is now positioned to the right of the API key field for a cleaner layout; DUMB and Agregarr API key fields are wider and easier to read.

Fix: Empty recommendation tag bubbles — items with low-confidence recently-released signals had a null reason label that rendered as a blank bubble on cards. Null and empty reasons are now filtered out.

API endpoint reference expanded — the reference table in Admin → General now documents all 28 public API endpoints across 6 categories: Recommendations & Discovery, Library & Search, Watchlist, Requests, Issues, and Notifications.

TMDB pipeline — region and language preferences (set in My Settings) are now applied to TMDB discover queries. Mature content toggle is stored server-side and controls TMDB include_adult at fetch time instead of only hiding cards client-side. Discover candidates are pre-fetched once per unique region+language+mature combination and shared across all matching users; per-user scoring runs every 28 minutes in the background so Explore loads instantly from a warm cache.

Shared TMDB detail cache — full item details (genres, keywords, cast, directors, ratings, trailers) are fetched and cached in the database once per item when the shared candidate pool is built. All users score against the same enriched data with no duplicate API calls. Per-user candidates from your watch history (TMDB recommendations, similar titles, person matches, keyword discovery, Plex related items) are still fetched per user and refreshed in the background.

Requested items in recommendations — items that have been requested but are not yet in the library now appear in recommendations with a Requested badge. If the item was requested by someone else, the request button becomes Notify Me — clicking it creates a follow entry and notifies you when the title becomes available.
Hide Requested toggle — a new toggle on the Explore page filters requested items out of all recommendation and trending sections server-side. Toggle state persists across sessions.

Accent-colored background gradient — all pages now show a subtle radial glow using the current accent color; spotlight from top center with a softer glow at the bottom. Updates in real-time when accent color is changed in the admin panel.
Fix: Default request service setting was not being passed to the frontend on explore and search pages, causing requests to always route to Overseerr regardless of admin settings.
Fix: Season rate limit bypass — the limit check now runs after deriving the real season count from TMDB so Agregarr requests for multi-season shows are counted correctly
Fix: Info modal credits username corrected to gage117
Fix: Approving, denying, editing, or deleting a queue request no longer scrolls to the top — all actions update the row in place
Fix: Resolving, closing, or deleting an issue no longer scrolls to the top — row is removed in place; delete uses the action modal instead of native confirm()
Fix: Queue items-per-page preference is now saved to localStorage and restored on page load
Fix: Queue and issues tables scroll horizontally on mobile instead of the whole page panning
Issues section added to the info modal
Bulk user settings — select multiple users in Admin → Users and apply the same settings override to all at once; each field has a three-state selector so only the fields you care about are changed
Season bubbles on TV requests — the request queue now shows individual season number chips (S1 S2 S3…) for each TV request
Request age — the queue Date column now shows relative age (e.g. 3 hours ago, 2 days ago) instead of an absolute date
Info modal credits — a Created by line at the bottom of the info modal links to the GitHub profiles of the project contributors

Fix: DUMB queue polling — approved requests now return media.status=3 (PROCESSING) so DUMB picks them up; added missing /movie/:id and /tv/:id detail endpoints DUMB uses for IMDb ID lookup; added type and media.media_type fields that DUMB accesses internally
Fix: Agregarr service user attribution — requests via the shim were attributed to the generic app user; fixed by reading the X-Api-User header Agregarr sends
Fix: Agregarr requests always pending — shim now calls getEffectiveAutoApprove() so requests auto-approve when the global or per-user setting allows it
Fix: Agregarr requests with non-TMDB IDs — requests whose TMDB ID returns 404 are now silently skipped with a clean 201 so Agregarr's sync continues
Fix: Agregarr rate limit response — hitting a per-user season or movie limit now returns a silent 201 instead of 403 so Agregarr's sync continues uninterrupted
Fix: Approve request with service=none — the approve endpoint now picks the best available service dynamically when service is none
Fix: Delete confirmation modal — replaced native confirm() with a custom in-page modal that supports a don't ask again checkbox backed by localStorage

DUMB request polling — DUMB connects to Diskovarr as its Overseerr source; enable in Admin → Connections → Riven → DUMB Integration; choose Pull (DUMB polls /api/v1/request) or Push (Diskovarr pushes to Riven on approval); DUMB marks content available via PUT /api/v1/media/:id/available
Search page autocomplete — TMDB suggestions on the search results page search bar, matching the nav bar behaviour
Fix: DUMB API key generated as 68 characters to satisfy Riven's Overseerr key length validation
Fix: Admin session cookie sameSite hardened from lax to strict
Riven/DUMB as a request service — Riven can now be selected as a request routing target alongside Overseerr, Radarr, and Sonarr; configure in Admin → Connections → Riven

Riven/DUMB torrent browser — DMM-style browser in Admin → Connections → Riven → Browse Torrents; search by title, view Torrentio results with RD cache status, inject chosen torrent into Riven in one click; includes season selector for TV and manual magnet paste fallback

Agregarr integration — Overseerr-compatible API shim at /api/v1/ lets Agregarr connect to Diskovarr as if it were Overseerr; enable in Admin → Connections → Agregarr; Agregarr creates service users automatically; their requests appear in the queue with a bot badge

Plex WebSocket — real-time library change notifications without Plex Pass; invalidates cache and checks fulfilled requests instantly when new content is added; auto-reconnects with exponential back-off
Fix: Admin connection tab eye/copy icon buttons now use solid filled icons, readable at all sizes

Plex webhook listener — Diskovarr now receives library.new events from Plex at POST /api/webhooks/plex, automatically invalidating the library cache and triggering fulfilled request checks when new content is added (requires Plex Pass)
Issue comments — users and admins can exchange comments on any issue in the issue detail modal; admin comments notify the reporter, user comments notify all admins
Discord independent webhook/bot toggles — both can now be enabled simultaneously, each with its own notification type list, embed poster toggle, and Save/Test button
Discord per-panel embed poster — webhook and bot each have their own embed poster toggle
Discord bot username and avatar settings moved into the Bot Token panel
Pushover notification sound — select from the full Pushover sound library or choose device default or silent
Pushover embed poster — attach the title's poster image to Pushover notifications
Pushover setup hints — help links to Pushover documentation under the API Token and User Key fields
Issue Comments notification type consolidated — the two separate issue comment checkboxes are merged into a single toggle across Discord and Pushover notification type lists
Admin broadcast notifications — type a message in Admin → Notifications and send it to all users at once via in-app bell, Discord, and Pushover simultaneously
Broadcast bell notifications open a modal showing the full message instead of navigating away
Last Visit column in admin Users tab — updates whenever a logged-in user browses the app, replacing the old Last Sync timestamp

Global requests disable toggle — the Request button is hidden site-wide and submissions are blocked when disabled; toggle is in Admin → Request Limits; individual users can be granted an override via per-user admin settings
Requests can now be submitted without a configured request service — the request appears in the queue and triggers notifications as normal
Landing page toggle: the Diskovarr Requests option is now disabled when the Requests tab is turned off

Request fulfilled notifications — when a requested title appears in the Plex library the requester receives a bell, Discord, and/or Pushover notification; also triggers instantly via Plex webhook (register in Plex → Settings → Webhooks)
Discord and Pushover notifications now group into one bundled message per event (matching the bell), with the poster from the first title embedded full-width
Discord and Pushover sends are skipped if the user already read the bell notification before the delivery window
Discord: issue_new and issue_updated now default to enabled for existing configs; embed poster defaults to on; issue_updated added to shared-channel webhook
Pushover: request_available type added to admin notification settings
Global nav search bar — always-visible search input on desktop between the nav tabs and bell; on mobile collapses to a magnifying glass icon that expands to a full-width overlay bar
Nav search shows TMDB autocomplete suggestions and navigates to the full search results page
Search results page: library items now show the full detail modal including Cast to TV and Report Issue; non-library items show the request modal
Back button on search page now returns to the previous page, or falls back to the configured landing page
Search results no longer hang when Plex TV library is slow — stale DB items are served immediately while a background sync runs
Elevated users can now view, approve, deny, edit, and delete all queue requests — not just their own
Changelog modal now shows only the three most recent versions

Issue reporting — report problems with library items directly from the detail modal; TV shows have a scope selector (Entire Series / Specific Season / Specific Episode) with season and episode number inputs
Issues page at /issues — users see their own reports with scope, status, and a Details button; admins and elevated users see all issues with poster, reporter, scope, status, and full detail view
Issue details modal — shows full description, scope breakdown, reporter (admin view), status, and any admin note
Admins can resolve or close issues with an optional note, displayed inline below the title and in the details modal
Notifications: issue_new sent to admins/elevated users when an issue is reported; issue_updated sent to the reporter when status changes; clicking either notification navigates to /issues
Fix: bell notifications now mark as read immediately when clicked, before navigating away
Discord and Pushover: issue_new and issue_updated added to notification type lists in admin settings
User settings and admin per-user settings: notify_issue_update (all users) and notify_issue_new (elevated users) toggles added
Issues link added to nav menu between Queue and Admin
Discord bot avatar upload — admins can upload a custom PNG/JPG/GIF (128×128 recommended, max 2 MB) for the bot's identity, with live preview; or enter a direct URL as an alternative
Discord/Pushover notification enable toggles — now rendered as styled slide-toggles at the top of each credential block in per-user admin settings
Owner lock — when a user is designated as the server owner, their elevated privileges toggle is locked, checked, and labelled in per-user admin settings
Recommendation pre-warming — after each library sync, user recommendation caches are rebuilt in the background so page loads are instant
Fix: recommendation pre-warm logger reference error corrected
Request queue page — users can view and manage their own requests; admins can review, approve, deny (with optional note), edit, or delete all requests
Per-user settings page — users can set region, language, notification preferences, and auto-request watchlist options
Admin per-user settings panel — full per-user controls for request limits, auto-approve overrides, elevated privileges, and notification preferences
Notification system — in-app bell with dropdown, Discord webhook (per-user and global), and Pushover push notifications
Elevated privileges role — admin-level flag per user with toggle in admin user panel
Auto-request from watchlist — global setting with per-user overrides for movies and TV shows
Changelog modal — version badge in the About panel opens a changelog history popup
Desktop user button redesigned — compact button matching the mobile FAB style with dropdown menu
Discord mode selector redesigned as a slide toggle (Webhook ↔ Bot Token) matching the site's toggle style
Bot mode shared-channel webhook option is now a toggle that reveals the URL field on demand
App Public URL setting moved to General Settings tab; enabling Discord bot mode without it set shows an error
Discord Server Invite Link — admins can set a non-expiring invite; users see a 'Join Server' prompt in their settings
Notification bell shows recent read notifications greyed out below unread; clicking 'Request available' navigates to the queue
Privileged user routing — elevated users receive admin-type notifications in addition to Plex admins
request_process_failed notification type added to Discord and Pushover agent type lists
Quality profile names persisted — saved profile name shown in the dropdown if Radarr/Sonarr is temporarily unreachable
Per-user admin settings page — action buttons for Re-sync Watched, Clear Watched, Clear Dismissals, Clear Requests
Themed checkboxes now consistent site-wide
